subject: ELA
title: Vocabulary & Word Meaning
description: Context clues, synonyms, antonyms, and figurative language.

questions.q: 'The ancient ruins were so fragile visitors could not touch them.' What does FRAGILE mean?
questions.explanation: Context: not allowed to touch = fragile = easily broken.

questions.q: What is a SYNONYM for BRAVE?
questions.explanation: Courageous and brave both mean not afraid of danger.

questions.q: What is the ANTONYM of GENEROUS?
questions.explanation: Stingy (unwilling to give) is the opposite of generous.

questions.q: 'It's raining cats and dogs.' What kind of language is this? (two words)
questions.explanation: This is an idiom — a type of figurative language.

questions.q: 'The wind howled through the trees.' This is an example of…
questions.explanation: Wind (non-human) is given a human action (howling).

questions.q: What does the root 'AUD' mean? (as in: audience, audible)
'She was so hungry she could eat a horse.' This is an example of…
questions.explanation: A wild exaggeration for effect = hyperbole.

questions.q: Complete the simile: 'As brave as a ___.'
questions.explanation: As brave as a lion — a common simile for courage.

questions.q: 'The pizza was a treasure chest of flavours.' This is an example of…
What does the suffix '-ful' mean in 'helpful' and 'joyful'?
questions.explanation: -ful means 'full of': helpful = full of help, joyful = full of joy.
